seoTitle: Connect Multiple Data Sources Into One Power BI Model
metaDescription: Learn how to connect multiple data sources in Power BI and combine them into a single unified model for cleaner cross-source reporting.
intro: Real business data rarely lives in one place, and Power BI's ability to combine multiple sources into a single model is what makes cross-departmental reporting possible. This guide covers selecting your data sources, creating relationships between them, and finalizing a unified model ready for reporting. It's aimed at analysts pulling together data from finance, marketing, or operations tools into one view.

steps.heading: Select your data sources
steps.body: Identify each data source you need to connect, whether that's databases, Excel files, or cloud services, and bring them into Power BI using the appropriate connector for each.

steps.heading: Create relationships between tables
steps.body: Define how the tables from different sources relate to each other, typically through shared keys like customer ID or date, so Power BI can join them correctly.

steps.heading: Finalize the unified model
steps.body: Review the relationships in Model view, check for cardinality issues or ambiguous paths, and clean up any duplicate or conflicting fields.

steps.heading: Apply best practices for data integration
steps.body: Keep a consistent naming convention across sources and use a proper date table so time-based analysis works cleanly across all combined data.

steps.heading: Review use cases and benefits
steps.body: A unified model lets you build reports that answer questions no single source could, like connecting sales figures to marketing spend or support tickets.

faq.question: Can Power BI combine data from completely different source types?
faq.answer: Yes, Power BI can connect to databases, files, and cloud services simultaneously and unify them into one data model as long as relationships between tables can be established.

faq.question: What causes relationship errors when connecting multiple sources?
faq.answer: Common causes include mismatched data types on key fields, missing shared keys, or ambiguous relationship paths between more than two tables.

faq.question: Do all data sources need to refresh on the same schedule?
faq.answer: Not necessarily, but keeping refresh schedules aligned helps avoid situations where one part of your unified model is more current than another.

faq.question: Is a unified model harder to maintain than separate reports?
faq.answer: It requires more upfront planning around relationships and naming, but it typically reduces long-term maintenance since you're managing one model instead of several disconnected reports.
